features.details: Review issue #115 to understand what the automation was attempting to process when the failure occurred. This will provide context on which code path triggered the error.

features.description: Locate the erroneous PullRequest.head attribute access
features.details: Search the AI Harness automation codebase for code that accesses the 'head' attribute on a PullRequest object. Identify all locations where this pattern occurs to understand the full scope of the issue.

locations_found.context: Attempting to access branch name from pull request object

findings.issue_summary: The code attempts to access 'head' attribute directly on PyGithub PullRequest object. In PyGithub, the correct attribute is 'head' which returns a PullRequestPart object, but the access pattern may fail when the head repository has been deleted or is inaccessible.
findings.recommended_fix: Use try-except blocks and check for None values before accessing nested attributes. Consider using pr.head.ref directly with proper error handling.

features.details: Examine the documentation for the GitHub API library being used (likely PyGithub or similar) to understand the correct structure of PullRequest objects and the proper way to access head/ref information.

head_attribute.description: Returns a PullRequestPart object containing info about the head branch

base_attribute.description: Returns a PullRequestPart object containing info about the base branch

attributes.name: repo
attributes.description: The repository object. Can be None if the fork was deleted

branch_name.notes: Always available, does not require repo to exist

owner_login.notes: Requires null check - repo can be None if fork was deleted

root_cause_analysis.cause: When a pull request comes from a forked repository that has been deleted, pr.head.repo returns None. Accessing .owner.login on None raises AttributeError.
scenarios: User creates PR from their fork
User deletes their fork after PR is created

pattern_1.description: Use pr.head.label instead of constructing from repo owner
pattern_1.benefit: Always works, even when fork is deleted

pattern_2.code: owner = pr.head.repo.owner.login if pr.head.repo else pr.base.repo.owner.login
pattern_2.benefit: Falls back to base repo owner

features.description: Implement fix for PullRequest attribute access
features.details: Update the code to correctly access pull request head information using the proper API/library methods. This may involve changing attribute names, using method calls, or accessing nested properties correctly.

features.description: Add defensive error handling and logging
features.details: Implement try-catch blocks around the pull request data access code with meaningful error messages. Add logging to help diagnose similar issues in the future.

features.description: Add unit tests for the fix
features.details: Create unit tests that verify the correct handling of PullRequest objects, including test cases that reproduce the original error condition and verify the fix works correctly.
After the fix is deployed, either re-trigger the automation for issue #115 or manually process it and close appropriately. Then close this issue #117.

overall_approach: Investigate the automation codebase to locate the erroneous PullRequest attribute access, identify the correct API/library usage pattern, and implement a fix with proper error handling. This requires examining the AI Harness automation code that processes GitHub issues and pull requests.
potential_risks: The PullRequest object may come from a different library/API than expected, requiring API documentation review
Fixing this may reveal additional issues in the automation pipeline
Issue #115 may need to be re-processed after the fix, requiring manual intervention
Changes to automation code could affect other automated workflows
